top_signals.title: Taiwan Drill Simulates Maritime ‘Quarantine’ by Chinese Forces
top_signals.summary: Taiwan has held tabletop military exercises to simulate its response to a potential maritime quarantine by China, National Security Council Deputy Secretary-General Lii Wen said at a briefing Thursday.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Georgia reduced wheat imports by 7%
top_signals.summary: From January to May 2026, Georgia imported 142,700 tons of wheat, a 7% decrease compared to the first five months of last year, according to the Zerno On-Line agency. Almost all of the wheat was supplied from Russia (128,400 tons). Kazakhstan supplied 7,800 tons, and the UAE supplied 6,400 tons. For almost 30 years of ...

top_signals.title: Half of UK wealth advisors say most clients’ crypto sits outside their oversight as 61% of European peers face firm restrictions: CoinShares
top_signals.summary: CoinShares survey found 52% of UK advisors report most clients' crypto exposure sits outside their oversight, with firm policy a key driver.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Korea and US launch council to drive $150b shipbuilding investment
top_signals.summary: A public-private consultative body will be launched to implement the $150 billion Korea-U.S. shipbuilding cooperation investment. The two countries allocated $150 billion to shipbuilding cooperation out of the $350 billion investment in the United States in Oct. last year. On the 25th, policy finance institutions including the U.S.-Korea Strategic Investment Corporation, The Export-Import Bank of ...
